# terrible_community_routeros_api_modify (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `data` (String) Data to ensure that is present for this path. Fields not provided will not be modified. If `.id` appears in an entry, it will be ignored.
- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against
- `path` (String) Path to query. An example value is `ip address`. This is equivalent to running modification commands in `/ip address` in the RouterOS CLI.

### Optional

- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `ensure_order` (Boolean) Whether to ensure the same order of the config as present in `data`. Requires `handle_absent_entries=remove`.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of name→value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `handle_absent_entries` (String) How to handle entries that are present in the current config, but not in `data`. `ignore` ignores them. `remove` removes them.
- `handle_entries_content` (String) For a single entry in `data`, this describes how to handle fields that are not mentioned in that entry, but appear in the actual config. If `ignore`, they are not modified. If `remove`, they are removed. If at least one cannot be removed, the module will fail. If `remove_as_much_as_possible`, all that can be removed will be removed. The ones that cannot be removed will be kept. Note that `remove` and `remove_as_much_as_possible` do not apply to write-only fields.
- `handle_read_only` (String) How to handle values passed in for read-only fields. If `ignore`, they are not passed to the API. If `validate`, the values are not passed for creation, and for updating they are compared to the value returned for the object. If they differ, the module fails. If `error`, the module will fail if read-only fields are provided.
- `handle_write_only` (String) How to handle values passed in for write-only fields. If `create_only`, they are passed on creation, and ignored for updating. If `always_update`, they are always passed to the API. This means that if such a value is present, the module will always result in `changed` since there is no way to validate whether the value actually changed. If `error`, the module will fail if write-only fields are provided.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `restrict` (String) Restrict operation to entries matching the following criteria. This can be useful together with `handle_absent_entries=remove` to operate on a subset of the values. For example, for `path=ip firewall filter`, you can set `restrict[].field=chain` and `restrict[].values=input` to restrict operation to the input chain, and ignore the forward and output chains.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
- `new_data` (String) A list of all elements for the current path after a change was made.
- `old_data` (String) A list of all elements for the current path before a change was made.
